attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who capitalizes on splits created by others in final 10km of summit finishes. Proven ability to outclimb breakaway companions on long observatory-style ascents when given space.
team_tactics: Minimal evidence of team support; appears to operate independently or within compromised team structures (Astana implosion 2020). Relies on positioning and personal reserves rather than domestique-led setups.
terrain_preference: Long, sustained summit finishes where superior pure climbing ability can be deployed in reduced groups. Observatory-style climbs ideal. Struggles with explosive, punchy efforts requiring repeated positioning battles.
vulnerability: Fragile GC campaigns with poor stage race durability (abandoned Giro 2020 before stage 2). Susceptible to positioning losses before key climbs. Team infrastructure weaknesses compound individual challenges.
key_patterns: Exceptional mechanical crisis management: recovered to lead group after double bike/wheel changes (2019)
Conservative pre-climb positioning observed across multiple Giro appearances, often moves backward in peloton
Stage race abandonment pattern: DNF Giro 2020 extraordinarily early, suggesting fragility beyond single bad day
Breakaway winner profile: won Vuelta stage from late split, not direct attacks from favorites group
form_trajectory: negative: catastrophic 2020 Giro abandonment before stage 2 suggests major form/health/team dysfunction, sharp decline from 2017-2019 competitiveness
